name: Orbit Notes
oneLineDescription: A lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items
whatItIs: Orbit Notes is a lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items. It helps small teams turn messy conversations into clear next steps. The primary use case is a team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates, and everyone can later search, filter, and track progress.
coreUserFlows: A team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates
Team members search, filter, and track progress on action items
Users sign up creating a new team or join an existing team via invite
Team leads manage team membership including inviting new members and changing roles

dataModel.purpose: Stores user accounts with email, password, and team membership
Stores team information and serves as the primary data scoping boundary
Containers for organizing notes within a team
Core content units that capture meeting discussions, decisions, and context
Tasks extracted from notes that can be assigned and tracked

crossCuttingPrinciples: All data access is scoped to the user's team with no cross-team leakage
Three-tier role-based authorization with Viewer, Contributor, and Team Lead permissions
A team must always have at least one Team Lead
Rich text support limited to headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, bullet lists, numbered lists
All features must work responsively on web browsers without requiring mobile-native apps
specSummary: Orbit Notes is designed as a lightweight team workspace specifically for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items, with the goal of helping small teams convert messy conversations into clear next steps. The application uses a single-team membership model where users belong to exactly one team, with role-based access control spanning Viewer, Contributor, and Team Lead roles. The core workflow centers around project-based note organization, where team leads and contributors create notes with rich text formatting during or after meetings, extract action items that can be assigned to teammates, and track progress through completion. The platform includes keyword search across all notes, an activity timeline showing recent team activity, and comprehensive team management features for inviting members and managing roles. Built on Next.js 14 with Supabase handling authentication, database, and real-time subscriptions, the application deliberately excludes multi-team membership, file attachments, real-time collaborative editing, notifications, and mobile-native apps to maintain focus on its core use case. The technical architecture emphasizes data isolation at the team level, with strict role-based permissions ensuring appropriate access control across all features.
